externalInputs.quantity: Fermi coupling constant divided by (hbar c)^3
externalInputs.sourceName: NIST CODATA 2022 recommended values of the fundamental physical constants
assumptions: Fermi coupling input is disjoint from W and Z mass target values for this repository's absolute-mass prediction gate
the input may set an electroweak scale but does not by itself define GeV per internal-mass-unit

derivedExternalScaleCandidates.notes: This is an external electroweak scale input, not yet a GeV-per-internal-mass-unit calibration.

blockReasons: no validated internal observable maps the Fermi-derived electroweak vacuum scale to internal-mass-unit
no target-independent internal weak coupling or mass-generation relation is validated for the W/Z modes
using v directly as GeV per internal-mass-unit would violate the Phase53 bridge contract
closureRequirements: derive a dimensionless internal bridge between the electroweak vacuum scale and the validated W/Z internal mass modes
prove the bridge excludes physical-w-boson-mass-gev and physical-z-boson-mass-gev
propagate both Fermi-input uncertainty and bridge uncertainty into GeV per internal-mass-unit

nextClosureSteps: search or derive an internal weak-coupling bridge for the validated W/Z modes
materialize the bridge as a replayable observable with finite uncertainty
combine the Fermi-derived external scale and bridge into GeV per internal-mass-unit
project W and Z absolute masses only after the bridge passes target-contamination checks
